description: ONOMA name-system known-answer tests (design: docs/design-names.md). Any implementation must reproduce these byte-for-byte to interoperate. An address is a post-quantum commitment to a key bundle, bech32m-encoded; per-epoch derivations make descriptors unenumerable. Pinned by the Rust reference suite (fanos-onoma/tests/conformance.rs).

address.comment: payload = version(0x01) || commitment(32); commitment = BLAKE3-256 under domain label 'FANOS-v1/onoma-addr' of the key bundle. Encoded bech32m (BIP-350) under the stable HRP 'onoma'; the canonical display name strips the 'onoma1' prefix and appends '.<tld>' (default '.fanos'). The mnemonic is the proquint rendering of the 32-byte commitment (16 groups), consonants 'bdfghjklmnprstvz', vowels 'aiou'.

derivations.comment: Per-epoch descriptor derivations. input = payload(version||commitment) || epoch as little-endian u64. lookup_key = BLAKE3 under 'FANOS-v1/onoma-lookup'; descriptor_key = BLAKE3 under 'FANOS-v1/onoma-enc'; lookup_point = MapToPoint('FANOS-v1/onoma-lookup', input) over the deployment field (canonical projective coordinate).

readable_names.comment: Readable names & subdomains (L-pet). A registry maps a top-level label to a Target (Delegate to a zone key, or a terminal Address). Subdomains resolve by walking delegated zones; a name terminating on a zone follows its apex '@' record. Names are strict lower-case ASCII LDH (letters/digits/hyphen).
